description_good_justification: README succinctly describes AI-HIL as a local MCP stdio server for safe, reproducible hardware-in-the-loop workflows: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil#ai-hil
interact_justification: README explains installation and use, links GitHub Issues for bugs/enhancements, and links CONTRIBUTING.md for contribution workflow: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil#feedback-and-contributions
contribution_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md explains development setup, pull request workflow, testing expectations, bug reports, hardware safety, and releases: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md
contribution_requirements_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md documents acceptable contribution requirements including focused changes, npm test, package checks, tests for behavior changes, and hardware safety boundaries: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md
floss_license_justification: AI-HIL is released under Apache-2.0, an OSI-approved FLOSS license: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/LICENSE
floss_license_osi_justification: Apache-2.0 is approved by the Open Source Initiative.
license_location_justification: The license is published as the top-level LICENSE file: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/LICENSE
documentation_basics_justification: README documents installation, project setup, supported hardware path, examples, expected outputs, MCP setup, COM stdio, safety model, and troubleshooting links: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil#quick-start
documentation_interface_justification: README documents the external CLI/MCP interface, configuration inputs, COM stdio usage, and structured JSON outputs: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil#expected-output https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil#mcp-client-configuration https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil#plain-com-text-stdio
sites_https_justification: Project, repository, issue, release, npm, and documentation URLs use HTTPS.
discussion_justification: GitHub Issues and Pull Requests provide searchable, URL-addressable project discussion without requiring proprietary client installation: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/issues https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/pulls
english_justification: README, SECURITY.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, issue templates, pull request template, and code comments are in English, and the project accepts English reports and comments.
maintained_justification: AI-HIL is actively maintained with recent commits, CI workflows, releases, issue templates, a security policy, and npm publication: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/releases
repo_public_justification: The source repository is publicly readable on GitHub: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil
repo_track_justification: Git tracks what changed, who changed it, and when: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/commits/master/
repo_interim_justification: The public GitHub repository contains normal development history and interim commits between releases, not only final release snapshots: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/commits/master/
repo_distributed_justification: The project uses git, a common distributed version control system, hosted on GitHub.
version_unique_justification: Each release has a unique version in package.json and a matching GitHub release/tag, for example v0.1.1: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/releases/tag/v0.1.1
version_semver_justification: AI-HIL uses Semantic Versioning; package.json, CHANGELOG.md, release tags, and docs/release-strategy.md document SemVer use: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/docs/release-strategy.md#versioning
version_tags_justification: Releases are identified with Git tags such as v0.1.1: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/releases/tag/v0.1.1
release_notes_justification: CHANGELOG.md and GitHub Releases provide human-readable release notes: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/releases
release_notes_vulns_justification: No publicly known AI-HIL run-time vulnerabilities with CVE or similar assignment have been fixed in published releases so far.
report_process_justification: Users can submit bug reports through GitHub Issues using the bug report template, and CONTRIBUTING.md describes what to include: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/issues/new/choose https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#bug-reports
report_tracker_justification: The project uses GitHub Issues to track individual bugs and enhancement requests: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/issues
report_responses_justification: The issue tracker is monitored. There are no known qualifying external bug reports in the last 2-12 months that lack maintainer acknowledgement.
enhancement_responses_justification: The issue tracker is monitored for enhancement requests. There are no known qualifying enhancement requests in the last 2-12 months that lack maintainer response.
report_archive_justification: GitHub Issues provide a public searchable archive for reports and responses: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/issues
vulnerability_report_process_justificati: SECURITY.md documents how to report vulnerabilities and what is in scope: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/SECURITY.md
vulnerability_report_private_justificati: SECURITY.md describes private vulnerability reporting through GitHub Security Advisories or direct maintainer email: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/SECURITY.md https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/security/advisories/new
vulnerability_report_response_justificat: No vulnerability reports have been received in the last 6 months. SECURITY.md states a 14-day acknowledgement target.
build_justification: The npm build script rebuilds the TypeScript project from source: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/package.json
build_common_tools_justification: The project uses common Node.js/TypeScript build tooling via npm scripts and tsc.
build_floss_tools_justification: The project builds with FLOSS tools: Node.js, npm, and TypeScript.
test_justification: The public test suite is in tests-ts and is run by npm test, which builds the project and executes runtime tests: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/package.json https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/tree/master/tests-ts
test_invocation_justification: The test suite is invocable using the standard npm test command for Node.js projects: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/package.json
test_most_justification: Automated tests cover core configuration, artifact validation, MCP tool, debugger, and stdio flows, but formal branch coverage is not yet measured.
test_continuous_integration_justificatio: GitHub Actions runs npm test on push and pull_request across Linux, macOS, and Windows: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/.github/workflows/ci.yml
test_policy_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md requires adding or updating tests when changing behavior: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#pull-requests
tests_are_added_justification: The repository includes behavior tests in tests-ts covering recent core functionality such as config loading, artifact validation, MCP tool calls, flashing paths, and stdio behavior: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/tree/master/tests-ts
tests_documented_added_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md documents that behavior changes should add or update tests: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#pull-requests
warnings_justification: TypeScript strict mode is enabled in tsconfig.json and npm test runs the TypeScript compiler: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/tsconfig.json https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/package.json
warnings_fixed_justification: CI runs npm test, which compiles TypeScript in strict mode; warnings/errors must be addressed for CI to pass: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/.github/workflows/ci.yml
warnings_strict_justification: The project uses TypeScript strict mode, forceConsistentCasingInFileNames, and CI compilation on every push and pull request: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/tsconfig.json
know_secure_design_justification: The maintainer documents AI-HIL's threat model, safety boundaries, mitigations, and secure development practices in docs/security-design.md and SECURITY.md: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/docs/security-design.md
know_common_errors_justification: docs/security-design.md and SECURITY.md document common error classes for this software, including raw debugger escape hatches, artifact-root bypass, COM-port bypass, MCP/serial stream confusion, destructive hardware actions, and log/config leaks: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/docs/security-design.md
crypto_published_justification: AI-HIL does not implement custom cryptographic protocols. It uses Node.js standard library SHA-256 for artifact metadata and relies on HTTPS, npm provenance, and GitHub attestations for delivery integrity: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/docs/security-design.md#cryptography-scope
crypto_call_justification: AI-HIL is not a cryptography implementation and calls Node.js standard library cryptography instead of reimplementing cryptographic primitives: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/docs/security-design.md#cryptography-scope
crypto_floss_justification: The cryptographic functionality AI-HIL relies on is implementable with FLOSS components: Node.js standard library crypto, HTTPS delivery, npm provenance, and GitHub artifact attestations.
crypto_keylength_justification: AI-HIL does not implement key agreement, encryption keys, TLS configuration, or user-configurable cryptographic key lengths.
crypto_working_justification: AI-HIL does not use broken cryptographic algorithms for security mechanisms; artifact metadata uses SHA-256 through Node.js standard library crypto: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/docs/security-design.md#cryptography-scope
crypto_weaknesses_justification: AI-HIL does not depend on SHA-1, MD5, single DES, RC4, or weak protocol modes for default security mechanisms.
crypto_pfs_justification: AI-HIL does not implement key agreement protocols or sessions protected by project-managed long-term cryptographic keys.
crypto_password_storage_justification: AI-HIL does not store passwords for authenticating external users.
crypto_random_justification: AI-HIL does not generate cryptographic keys or nonces.
delivery_mitm_justification: Source, releases, npm package metadata, and project documentation are delivered over HTTPS, and releases use npm provenance and GitHub artifact attestations: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil#distribution
delivery_unsigned_justification: The project does not publish a workflow that retrieves cryptographic hashes over HTTP and trusts them without a signature. Distribution uses HTTPS plus npm provenance and GitHub attestations: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil#distribution
vulnerabilities_fixed_60_days_justificat: There are no known unpatched AI-HIL vulnerabilities of medium or higher severity older than 60 days. CI runs npm audit for production dependencies and Dependency Review checks pull requests: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/.github/workflows/ci.yml https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/.github/workflows/dependency-review.yml
vulnerabilities_critical_fixed_justifica: There are no known critical AI-HIL vulnerabilities. SECURITY.md defines reporting and supported-version handling for security fixes: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/SECURITY.md
no_leaked_credentials_justification: The public repository has no known valid private credentials. CONTRIBUTING.md and SECURITY.md instruct contributors to sanitize local config, logs, reports, and secrets before sharing: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/SECURITY.md
static_analysis_justification: CodeQL static analysis for JavaScript and TypeScript runs on push, pull_request, and weekly schedule: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/.github/workflows/codeql.yml
static_analysis_common_vulnerabilities_j: CodeQL includes JavaScript/TypeScript security queries for common vulnerability patterns: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/.github/workflows/codeql.yml
static_analysis_fixed_justification: There are no known confirmed medium or higher severity exploitable CodeQL findings left unfixed. CodeQL runs before releases and on pull requests: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/.github/workflows/codeql.yml
static_analysis_often_justification: CodeQL runs on push, pull_request, and weekly schedule: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/.github/workflows/codeql.yml
dynamic_analysis_justification: npm test executes runtime tests before publish and release, exercising configuration, artifact handling, MCP tools, debugger flows, and stdio behavior: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/package.json https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/tree/master/tests-ts
dynamic_analysis_unsafe_justification: AI-HIL is implemented in TypeScript/JavaScript and does not include C, C++, or another memory-unsafe implementation language in the project code.
dynamic_analysis_enable_assertions_justi: The runtime test suite uses node:assert/strict assertions throughout tests-ts/run-tests.mjs: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/tests-ts/run-tests.mjs
dynamic_analysis_fixed_justification: There are no known confirmed medium or higher severity exploitable vulnerabilities discovered by dynamic tests. Release and contributor docs require tests before release and pull requests: https://github.com/hp-8472/aihil/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md
